author: ojiudezue
title: Accept 10-minute DB write warmup period after restart; defer deeper fixes to backlog
summary: After v4.2.6 startup deferral + jitter, DB write worker timeouts reduced from t=0 (immediate, 15-min HA startup) to t=5min (burst at deferred mark, 10-min warmup). System stabilizes fully after ~10 minutes. Accepting this as the current steady state. Deeper fixes (non-blocking writes, write batching, larger jitter) deferred to backlog as tech debt.

decisions.decision: Accept 10-minute startup warmup with transient DB write timeouts as current behavior
alternatives_considered: Option A: Increase jitter window from 60s to 240s — spreads writes more but some rooms write during early startup contention. Partial fix, diminishing returns.
Option B (chosen): Accept current state. Errors are transient, non-destructive, self-healing. No user-facing impact after 10 minutes.

Option C: Non-blocking fire-and-forget writes — callers don't await the write queue. Eliminates timeouts entirely but changes the write ownership model and error handling. Medium-risk architectural change.
Option D: Write batching — group multiple writes into single transactions. Reduces write count by ~70% but requires a coordinator-level batch timer. High-risk architectural change.
Option E: Increase 35s timeout to 120s during startup — masks the problem, rejected.
decisions.why: The system stabilizes after 10 minutes. The transient errors at t=5min are caused by 31 rooms' deferred writes converging within a 60s window plus census grace expiry plus midnight snapshot catch-up. The write worker processes all queued writes — they complete, just after the caller's 35s timeout fires. No data is lost. No user-facing impact. Further optimization requires architectural changes to the write queue (non-blocking or batching) that carry real risk for diminishing returns on a problem that only manifests during restart.
decisions.implications: Users will see 5-10 DB write timeout errors in the log after every HA restart. These are harmless but look alarming. Should be documented in a known-issues section. If the room count grows significantly (50+), the warmup period will lengthen and deeper fixes will be needed.
decisions.revisit_trigger: If room count exceeds 40, if warmup period exceeds 15 minutes, if DB write timeouts occur during steady-state operation (not just startup), or if a user reports HA unresponsiveness lasting more than 10 minutes after restart.

refs.what_prompted_this: v4.2.6 deferral reduced startup contention but 5-minute mark still shows write timeouts. After analysis, determined remaining errors are transient and self-healing.
